channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Ebola death toll reaches 600, as new cases suspected in other parts of Congo
item.description: New suspected cases of Ebola have been reported in parts of Congo that were previously unaffected, the government said, signaling the continued spread of the disease beyond the epicenter in Ituri.
item.content:encoded:
New suspected cases of Ebola have been reported in parts of Congo that were previously unaffected, the government said, signaling the continued spread of the disease beyond the epicenter in Ituri.

(Image credit: Dirole Lotsima Dieudonne)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange intensifying fire across Mideast, threatening ceasefire deal
item.description: Back-and-forth attacks have repeatedly threatened the ceasefire, but Thursday's appeared bigger all around.
item.content:encoded:
Back-and-forth attacks have repeatedly threatened the ceasefire, but Thursday's appeared bigger all around.

(Image credit: Hasan Salavati)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Former U.S. envoy reacts to Iran strikes and NATO summit
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to NATO Nicholas Burns regarding this week's developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict -- and the NATO summit this week.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to NATO Nicholas Burns regarding this week's developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict -- and the NATO summit this week.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Where are Iran and Ukraine wars headed after NATO summit?
item.description: Fresh strikes between Iran and the United States and a tense ceasefire scrapped — Is the war with Iran back on?
item.content:encoded: Fresh strikes between Iran and the United States and a tense ceasefire scrapped — Is the war with Iran back on?
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: Graham Platner ends Senate bid. And, why Nolan Wells' death captured national attention
item.description: Democrat Graham Platner ended his bid last night for U.S. Senate. And, prominent civil rights attorney Ben Crump has been enlisted to help get answers in the July Fourth weekend death of Nolan Wells.
item.content:encoded:
Democrat Graham Platner ended his bid last night for U.S. Senate. And, prominent civil rights attorney Ben Crump has been enlisted to help get answers in the July Fourth weekend death of Nolan Wells.

(Image credit: Laura Brett)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: How England's class divide shaped Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s likely next prime minister
item.description: As mayor of Manchester, Andy Burnham brought growth to the postindustrial city. Can he scale that nationally as the next prime minister?
item.content:encoded:
As mayor of Manchester, Andy Burnham brought growth to the postindustrial city. Can he scale that nationally as the next prime minister?

(Image credit: Jon Super)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Egypt complains officials were biased in World Cup loss to Argentina
item.description: The Egyptian Football Association (EFA) said Wednesday it "cannot remain silent" after what it believes was unfair and biased officiating in Egypt's 3-2 round of 16 loss against Argentina on Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
The Egyptian Football Association (EFA) said Wednesday it "cannot remain silent" after what it believes was unfair and biased officiating in Egypt's 3-2 round of 16 loss against Argentina on Tuesday.

(Image credit: Erik S. Lesser)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump wraps NATO summit on a positive note, after meeting Zelenskyy
item.description: President Trump capped a NATO summit in Turkey meeting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, and saying that the U.S. will give Ukraine a license to make Patriot air defense systems.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump capped a NATO summit in Turkey meeting Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, and saying that the U.S. will give Ukraine a license to make Patriot air defense systems.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The U.S. launches new airstrikes on Iran and Tehran fires back at Gulf Arab states
item.description: The United States launched new airstrikes against Iran early Thursday, and Tehran responded by targeting U.S.-allied Mideast countries, raising fears of resuming war.
item.content:encoded:
The United States launched new airstrikes against Iran early Thursday, and Tehran responded by targeting U.S.-allied Mideast countries, raising fears of resuming war.

(Image credit: Khalil Hamra)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In a West Bank cave, Israelis and Palestinians hold an out-of-the-ordinary lunch
item.description: In the occupied West Bank, Israeli settlers keep seizing land in areas where Palestinians have lived for generations. The conflict even reaches caves in remote corners of the territory.
item.content:encoded:
In the occupied West Bank, Israeli settlers keep seizing land in areas where Palestinians have lived for generations. The conflict even reaches caves in remote corners of the territory.

(Image credit: Greg Myre)
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre
